children.label: i
children.text: a rule promulgated by the Administrator;

children.label: ii
children.text: a consent agreement entered into by the Administrator; or

children.label: iii
children.text: an order issued by the Administrator.

children.heading: Chemical substances found not to present an unreasonable risk or restricted
children.label: B Chemical substances found not to present an unreasonable risk or restricted
i
children.text: for which the determination described in is made, consistent with the scope of the risk evaluation under section 2605(b)(4)(D) of this title; orsection 2605(i)(1) of this title1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: ii
children.text: for which a final rule is promulgated under , after the effective date of the rule issued under for the chemical substance, consistent with the scope of the risk evaluation under section 2605(b)(4)(D) of this title.section 2605(a) of this titlesection 2605(a) of this title1

children.heading: Significant new use
children.label: C Significant new use
children.text: A statute or administrative action requiring the notification of a use of a chemical substance that the Administrator has specified as a significant new use and for which the Administrator has required notification pursuant to a rule promulgated under .section 2604 of this title

children.heading: Effective date of preemption
children.label: 2 Effective date of preemption
children.text: Under this subsection, Federal preemption of statutes and administrative actions applicable to specific chemical substances shall not occur until the effective date of the applicable action described in paragraph (1) taken by the Administrator.

children.heading: New statutes, criminal penalties, or administrative actions creating prohibitions or other restrictions
children.label: b New statutes, criminal penalties, or administrative actions creating prohibitions or other restrictions

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in subsections (c), (d), (e), (f), and (g), beginning on the date on which the Administrator defines the scope of a risk evaluation for a chemical substance under and ending on the date on which the deadline established pursuant to for completion of the risk evaluation expires, or on the date on which the Administrator publishes the risk evaluation under , whichever is earlier, no State or political subdivision of a State may establish a statute, criminal penalty, or administrative action prohibiting or otherwise restricting the manufacture, processing, distribution in commerce, or use of such chemical substance that is a high-priority substance designated under .section 2605(b)(4)(D) of this titlesection 2605(b)(4)(G) of this titlesection 2605(b)(4)(C) of this titlesection 2605(b)(1)(B)(i) of this title

children.heading: Effect of subsection
children.label: 2 Effect of subsection
children.text: This subsection does not restrict the authority of a State or political subdivision of a State to continue to enforce any statute enacted, criminal penalty assessed, or administrative action taken, prior to the date on which the Administrator defines and publishes the scope of a risk evaluation under .section 2605(b)(4)(D) of this title
with respect to subsection (a)(1)(A), the chemical substances or category of chemical substances subject to a rule, order, or consent agreement under section 2603, 2604, or 2605 of this title;

with respect to subsection (b), the hazards, exposures, risks, and uses or conditions of use of such chemical substances included in the scope of the risk evaluation pursuant to ;section 2605(b)(4)(D) of this title

with respect to subsection (a)(1)(B), the hazards, exposures, risks, and uses or conditions of use of such chemical substances included in any final action the Administrator takes pursuant to section 2605(a) or 2605(i)(1) of this title; or

with respect to subsection (a)(1)(C), the uses of such chemical substances that the Administrator has specified as significant new uses and for which the Administrator has required notification pursuant to a rule promulgated under .section 2604 of this title

children.heading: No preemption of statutes and administrative actions
children.label: 1 No preemption of statutes and administrative actions
i
children.text: is adopted or authorized under the authority of any other Federal law or adopted to satisfy or obtain authorization or approval under any other Federal law;

children.label: ii
children.text: implements a reporting, monitoring, or other information obligation for the chemical substance not otherwise required by the Administrator under this chapter or required under any other Federal law;

children.label: I
children.text: imposes a restriction on the manufacture, processing, distribution in commerce, or use of a chemical substance; and

children.label: aa
children.text: addresses the same hazards and exposures, with respect to the same conditions of use as are included in the scope of the risk evaluation published pursuant to , but is inconsistent with the action of the Administrator; orsection 2605(b)(4)(D) of this title

children.label: bb
children.text: would cause a violation of the applicable action by the Administrator under section 2604 or 2605 of this title; or

children.label: iv
children.text: subject to subparagraph (B), is identical to a requirement prescribed by the Administrator.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The penalties and other sanctions applicable under a law of a State or political subdivision of a State in the event of noncompliance with the identical requirement shall be no more stringent than the penalties and other sanctions available to the Administrator under .section 2615 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: a State or political subdivision of a State may not assess a penalty for a specific violation for which the Administrator has assessed an adequate penalty under ; andsection 2615 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: if a State or political subdivision of a State has assessed a penalty for a specific violation, the Administrator may not assess a penalty for that violation in an amount that would cause the total of the penalties assessed for the violation by the State or political subdivision of a State and the Administrator combined to exceed the maximum amount that may be assessed for that violation by the Administrator under .section 2615 of this title

children.heading: Applicability to certain rules or orders
children.label: 2 Applicability to certain rules or orders

children.heading: Prior rules and orders
children.label: A Prior rules and orders
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as modifying the preemptive effect under this section, as in effect on the day before the effective date of the Frank R. Lautenberg Chemical Safety for the 21st Century Act, of any rule or order promulgated or issued under this chapter prior to that effective date.

children.heading: Certain chemical substances and mixtures
children.label: B Certain chemical substances and mixtures
children.text: With respect to a chemical substance or mixture for which any rule or order was promulgated or issued under prior to the effective date of the Frank R. Lautenberg Chemical Safety for the 21st Century Act with respect to manufacturing, processing, distribution in commerce, use, or disposal of the chemical substance or mixture, nothing in this section shall be construed as modifying the preemptive effect of this section as in effect prior to the enactment of the Frank R. Lautenberg Chemical Safety for the 21st Century Act of any rule or order that is promulgated or issued with respect to such chemical substance or mixture under after that effective date, unless the latter rule or order is with respect to a chemical substance or mixture containing a chemical substance and follows a designation of that chemical substance as a high-priority substance under , the identification of that chemical substance under , or the selection of that chemical substance for risk evaluation under .section 2605 of this titlesection 2605 of this titlesection 2605(b)(1)(B)(i) of this titlesection 2605(b)(2)(A) of this titlesection 2605(b)(4)(E)(iv)(II) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: be construed to preempt or otherwise affect the authority of a State or political subdivision of a State to continue to enforce any action taken or requirement imposed or requirement enacted relating to a specific chemical substance before , under the authority of a law of the State or political subdivision of the State that prohibits or otherwise restricts manufacturing, processing, distribution in commerce, use, or disposal of a chemical substance; orApril 22, 2016

children.label: B
children.text: be construed to preempt or otherwise affect any action taken pursuant to a State law that was in effect on .August 31, 2003

children.heading: Effect of subsection
children.label: 2 Effect of subsection
children.text: This subsection does not affect, modify, or alter the relationship between Federal law and laws of a State or political subdivision of a State pursuant to any other Federal law.

children.label: A
children.text: compelling conditions warrant granting the waiver to protect health or the environment;

children.label: B
children.text: compliance with the proposed requirement of the State or political subdivision of the State would not unduly burden interstate commerce in the manufacture, processing, distribution in commerce, or use of a chemical substance;

children.label: C
children.text: compliance with the proposed requirement of the State or political subdivision of the State would not cause a violation of any applicable Federal law, rule, or order; and

children.label: i
children.text: consistent with the best available science;

children.label: ii
children.text: using supporting studies conducted in accordance with sound and objective scientific practices; and

children.label: iii
children.text: based on the weight of the scientific evidence.

children.label: i
children.text: compliance with the proposed requirement of the State or political subdivision of the State would not unduly burden interstate commerce in the manufacture, processing, distribution in commerce, or use of a chemical substance;

children.label: ii
children.text: compliance with the proposed requirement of the State or political subdivision of the State would not cause a violation of any applicable Federal law, rule, or order; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the State or political subdivision of the State has a concern about the chemical substance or use of the chemical substance based in peer-reviewed science; or

children.label: B
children.text: no later than the date that is 18 months after the date on which the Administrator has initiated the prioritization process for a chemical substance under the rule promulgated pursuant to , or the date on which the Administrator publishes the scope of the risk evaluation for a chemical substance under , whichever is sooner, the State or political subdivision of the State has enacted a statute or proposed or finalized an administrative action intended to prohibit or otherwise restrict the manufacture, processing, distribution in commerce, or use of the chemical substance.section 2605(b)(1)(A) of this titlesection 2605(b)(4)(D) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: not later than 180 days after the date on which an application under paragraph (1) is submitted; and

children.label: B
children.text: not later than 110 days after the date on which an application under paragraph (2) is submitted.

children.heading: Failure to make a determination
children.label: 4 Failure to make a determination
children.text: If the Administrator fails to make a determination under paragraph (3)(B) during the 110-day period beginning on the date on which an application under paragraph (2) is submitted, the statute or administrative action of the State or political subdivision of the State that was the subject of the application shall not be considered to be an existing statute or administrative action for purposes of subsection (b) by reason of the failure of the Administrator to make a determination.

children.heading: Notice and comment
children.label: 5 Notice and comment
children.text: Except in the case of an application approved under paragraph (9), the application of a State or political subdivision of a State under this subsection shall be subject to public notice and comment.

children.label: A
children.text: considered to be a final agency action; and

children.label: B
children.text: subject to judicial review.

children.heading: Duration of waivers
children.label: 7 Duration of waivers
children.text: A waiver granted under paragraph (2) or approved under paragraph (9) shall remain in effect until such time as the Administrator publishes the risk evaluation under .section 2605(b) of this title

children.heading: Judicial review of waivers
children.label: 8 Judicial review of waivers
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date on which the Administrator makes a determination on an application of a State or political subdivision of a State under paragraph (1) or (2), any person may file a petition for judicial review in the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit, which shall have exclusive jurisdiction over the determination.

children.heading: Automatic approval
children.label: A Automatic approval
children.text: If the Administrator fails to meet the deadline established under paragraph (3)(B), the application of a State or political subdivision of a State under paragraph (2) shall be automatically approved, effective on the date that is 10 days after the deadline.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: B Requirements
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (6), approval of a waiver application under subparagraph (A) for failure to meet the deadline under paragraph (3)(B) shall not be considered final agency action or be subject to judicial review or public notice and comment.

children.heading: No preemption of common law or statutory causes of action for civil relief or criminal conduct
children.label: 1 No preemption of common law or statutory causes of action for civil relief or criminal conduct

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Nothing in this chapter, nor any amendment made by the Frank R. Lautenberg Chemical Safety for the 21st Century Act, nor any standard, rule, requirement, standard of performance, risk evaluation, or scientific assessment implemented pursuant to this chapter, shall be construed to preempt, displace, or supplant any State or Federal common law rights or any State or Federal statute creating a remedy for civil relief, including those for civil damage, or a penalty for a criminal conduct.

children.heading: Clarification of no preemption
children.label: B Clarification of no preemption
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter, nothing in this chapter, nor any amendments made by the Frank R. Lautenberg Chemical Safety for the 21st Century Act, shall preempt or preclude any cause of action for personal injury, wrongful death, property damage, or other injury based on negligence, strict liability, products liability, failure to warn, or any other legal theory of liability under any State law, maritime law, or Federal common law or statutory theory.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Nothing in this chapter, nor any amendments made by the Frank R. Lautenberg Chemical Safety for the 21st Century Act, nor any rules, regulations, requirements, risk evaluations, scientific assessments, or orders issued pursuant to this chapter shall be interpreted as, in either the plaintiff’s or defendant’s favor, dispositive in any civil action.

children.heading: Authority of courts
children.label: B Authority of courts
children.text: This chapter does not affect the authority of any court to make a determination in an adjudicatory proceeding under applicable State or Federal law with respect to the admission into evidence or any other use of this chapter or rules, regulations, requirements, standards of performance, risk evaluations, scientific assessments, or orders issued pursuant to this chapter.
